A01.name: Broken Access Control
A01.description: Restrictions on authenticated users are not properly enforced.
A01.impact: Attackers can access sensitive data or perform unauthorized actions

A02.name: Cryptographic Failures
A02.description: Sensitive data exposure due to weak or missing encryption.
A02.impact: Attackers may steal credentials, credit cards, or personal data

A03.name: Injection
A03.description: Untrusted input is interpreted as code or commands.
A03.impact: Attackers can execute commands or manipulate databases

A04.name: Insecure Design
A04.description: Security flaws due to poor system design.
A04.impact: System can be exploited due to architectural weaknesses

A06.name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
A06.description: Using libraries or software with known vulnerabilities.
A06.impact: Attackers exploit known CVEs in outdated software

A07.name: Identification and Authentication Failures
A07.description: Authentication systems are weak or broken.
A07.impact: Attackers can take over user accounts

A08.name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
A08.description: Code and infrastructure do not verify integrity.
A08.impact: Attackers inject malicious code into applications

A09.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
A09.description: Security events are not logged or monitored properly.
A09.impact: Attacks go undetected for long periods

A10.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
A10.description: Server fetches remote resources without validating URLs.
A10.impact: Attackers access internal systems or cloud metadata
